issues.issue: Both options are grammatically valid Indonesian -- 'Ibu makan pisang' and 'Ibu memakan pisang' are both correct. The distinction is register/formality, not correctness. Learner may feel tricked if they know the me- form.
issues.fix: Admin review: explanation is clear about A1 level expectation. Consider whether this tests the right thing (base form preference) vs. implying memakan is wrong.

issues.issue: Distractors (mau, boleh, bisa) are modal verbs, not negation particles. The functional category differs from the correct answers (belum/tidak are negations). This tests negation-identification rather than belum-vs-tidak discrimination.
issues.fix: Admin review: acceptable since only two negation forms exist at this level and having both as options + 2 modals is the best available design. The explanations clearly teach the belum/tidak contrast.
